screenshot_note: Chromium headless unavailable in this environment (pthread quota exceeded). Visual review based on code + deployed CSS bundle + prior captures from 20:06 session. No new layout changes detected since prior screenshots.
fixed_since_last: localStorage JSON.parse for vibe history now wrapped in try-catch ✅ (was crashing in Safari private mode)
still_open_from_last: confetti only fires for first shipped worker in simultaneous completions
wall clock setInterval not synced to next minute boundary — drifts up to 59s
browser notification API for error states (feature)
scattered z-index values — no custom property scale

suggestions.title: useRef called inside conditional block — Rules of Hooks violation
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 304: `const gradId = useRef(...)` is called inside `if (workerId === 'luna')`. This violates the Rules of Hooks (hooks must not be called conditionally). React may reorder or skip hooks between renders, especially in StrictMode, hot-reload, or if the component tree structure changes. Fix: hoist the useRef call to the top of CharacterAvatar (before any conditionals), but only use the value when workerId is 'luna'. Alternatively, split Luna into its own memoized sub-component. Bonus: this also removes the dependency on the mutable module-level _gradIdx counter.

suggestions.title: keyboard activation of office characters crashes on touch+keyboard devices
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 602: `onKeyDown={handleClick ? (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); handleClick() } } : undefined}` calls `handleClick()` with no arguments. Inside handleClick (line 585), if `window.matchMedia('(hover: none)').matches` is true (Surface tablets, touch-first devices with keyboards), the code reaches `e.stopPropagation()` where `e` is `undefined` — TypeError crash. Fix: pass the keyboard event to handleClick, or guard with optional chaining: `e?.stopPropagation()`.

suggestions.title: Module-level _gradIdx counter still breaks StrictMode and hot-reload
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 3: `let _gradIdx = 0` — increments every time CharacterAvatar renders, never resets. React StrictMode double-invokes renders, causing IDs like `luna-hair-luna-2` vs `luna-hair-luna-3` between invocations, making the SVG gradient reference point to a non-existent ID and rendering Luna's hair as a solid color fallback. Replace with `useId()` (React 18+): `const gradId = useId()` hoisted unconditionally at the top of CharacterAvatar, and just use `gradId` inside the Luna branch.

suggestions.title: Confetti still only fires for first worker in simultaneous completions
suggestions.description: App.jsx line ~833: `const worker = justShipped[0]` — when two workers complete in the same poll cycle (common if polling catches up after a tab switch), only the first one in the array gets the confetti and toast. Carlos ships 3 features, Maya notices only Carlos got celebrated. Fix: iterate `justShipped` array and stagger each confetti+toast trigger by 300ms: `justShipped.forEach((w, i) => setTimeout(() => triggerCelebration(w), i * 300))`. Requires refactoring completion toast to support a queue.

suggestions.title: Broken mobile CSS selectors from 'Luna Review #41' fix
suggestions.description:
App.css lines 1071-1086: the mobile header fix under `@media (max-width: 480px)` references three non-existent selectors. `.tab-content` is never used in JSX (the tab panels use inline `role=tabpanel` divs with no class). `.header-subtitle` is never used in JSX (the subtitle is just `

` inside `.header-title`). `.header h1` has specificity 0-1-1 and is overridden by the existing `.header-title h1` rule at specificity 0-2-1 from the 600px breakpoint — so the h1 never shrinks to 1rem on 480px screens as intended. Fix all three selectors: `.tab-panel` (or just remove if unused), `.header-title p`, `.header-title h1` respectively.

suggestions.title: Activity log 'Last X events' count ignores active filters
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1154: `Last {Math.min(activityLog.length, 20)} events` always shows the pre-filter total. If the user filters by 'Errors' and only 3 error entries exist, the label still shows 'Last 20 events' — a lie. Calculate the count AFTER applying both the type filter and the worker filter: `const filteredCount = activityLog.filter(...typeFilter).filter(...workerFilter).length` and use `Math.min(filteredCount, 20)` in the label.

suggestions.title: O(n×m) activity filter inside render loop not memoized
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1104, 1127, 1140: `activityLog.filter(e => e.worker === w.name).slice(-3)` is called for every worker card on every render. With 10 workers and 50 activity log entries, this is 500 comparisons per render cycle, triggered by the 60s poll AND the 60s time-tick AND any state change. Add `useMemo` to pre-compute a map of activity entries keyed by worker name: `const activityByWorker = useMemo(() => activityLog.reduce((acc, e) => { (acc[e.worker] ??= []).push(e); return acc }, {}), [activityLog])` and pass slices from that map.

suggestions.title: Stats bar grid overflows silently when error + stuck cards both present
suggestions.description: App.css: `.stats-bar` uses `grid-template-columns: repeat(5, 1fr)` (7 columns at 900px+ breakpoints). When `errorCount > 0` AND `stuckWorkers.length > 0`, the StatsBar renders 7 cards against a 5-column grid, causing the 6th and 7th to flow to a second row without any alignment treatment. This looks broken: the last row has 2 cards at full width instead of 5. Fix: use `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(160px, 1fr))` or conditionally add a CSS modifier class when extra cards are present.

suggestions.title: Wall clock still drifts up to 59 seconds on initial mount
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 880: `setInterval(() => setTime(new Date()), 60000)` starts immediately on mount, not aligned to the next clock minute. If mounted at :45, the clock shows the correct time but won't update until :45 of the next minute — it could be 59 seconds stale. Fix: calculate initial delay to next minute boundary and use a setTimeout → setInterval chain: `const msUntilNextMinute = 60000 - (Date.now() % 60000); setTimeout(() => { setTime(new Date()); const id = setInterval(...); }, msUntilNextMinute)`.

suggestions.title: Add CSS containment to ambient particle containers
suggestions.description: App.css: The `.ambient-particles` container (12 particles in App, 7 in Office = 19 total) runs continuous CSS animation on all particles even when offscreen. Add `contain: strict` or `contain: layout paint` to `.ambient-particles` so the browser can skip layout recalculation outside this boundary during each animation frame. Also consider reducing particle count to 8 — at opacity 0.15, the visual difference between 12 and 8 is imperceptible, but the paint savings aren't.

suggestions.title: Browser Notification API for critical status alerts
suggestions.description: Still not implemented since review #22. When a worker enters 'error' status or vibe shifts to 'on-fire', active-tab monitoring is useless if the user is in another tab. Add an opt-in 🔔 toggle in the header that calls `Notification.requestPermission()` and sends browser notifications on error state transitions. Gate the feature behind permission check and persist opt-in preference to localStorage under 'bloberto-notify'. Estimated user value: high for any real monitoring use case.

suggestions.title: Dead CSS classes polluting mobile breakpoints
suggestions.description: App.css contains references to classes that do not exist in any JSX: `.tab-content` (referenced in 480px breakpoint), `.header-subtitle` (referenced in 480px breakpoint). Also `.progress-wrapper {}` at line ~340 is an empty rule block. Clean up: grep all CSS selectors against JSX usage and remove orphans. Prevents confusion for future maintainers who will spend 20 minutes looking for `.header-subtitle` in JSX and finding nothing.

suggestions.title: Activity log export button (JSON/CSV)
suggestions.description: Still not implemented since review #22. A small 'Export ↓' button next to 'Last N events' that downloads the current filtered activity array as JSON or CSV. Useful for sprint retrospectives. Implemented as a Blob download: `URL.createObjectURL(new Blob([JSON.stringify(entries)]))`. Total: ~5 lines. Assignee still Maya.

suggestions.title: Footer hardcodes version string instead of reading package.json
suggestions.description: App.jsx footer renders `v1.0.0-chaos` as a literal string. The actual package.json has `"version": "0.0.0"`. These will inevitably diverge. In a Vite project, import the version via `import.meta.env` + define plugin, or import from package.json directly: `import { version } from '../package.json'` and render `v${version}`. Or just pick one source of truth and use it.

suggestions.title: Inter font still not loaded — silent system-font fallback
suggestions.description: Still not implemented since review #22. `font-family: 'Inter'` is declared in index.css but no @font-face or CDN link loads it. On Linux and older Windows, the app silently renders in system-ui (often Segoe UI on Windows, DejaVu Sans on Linux), causing cross-platform inconsistency. Add `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.bunny.net">` + `<link href="https://fonts.bunny.net/css?family=inter:400,600,700,800" rel="stylesheet">` to index.html. Bunny.net is GDPR-compliant (unlike Google Fonts).
